system.name: Skeletal System
system.shortDescription: 206 bones forming the body's structural framework. Plus joints, cartilage, ligaments, bone marrow (hematopoiesis), tooth enamel. Saturn's primary domain.

primaryStructures: Spine (33 vertebrae — 7 cervical, 12 thoracic, 5 lumbar, 5 fused sacral, 4 fused coccygeal)
Upper extremity (clavicle, scapula, humerus, radius, ulna, carpals, metacarpals, phalanges)
Lower extremity (femur, patella, tibia, fibula, tarsals, metatarsals, phalanges)
Bone marrow (red — hematopoiesis; yellow — fat storage)
anatomy.vertebralCorrelates: ENTIRE SPINAL COLUMN is part of this system. Per Cornell + osteopathic tradition: each vertebral level affects downstream organs through its emerging nerve roots. C1 (atlas) - sometimes considered most influential single bone.
anatomy.nervePlexus: All major plexus pass through skeletal structure. Vertebral foramina protect nerve roots.

astrologicalRulership.primaryPlanetRationale: Saturn rules structure, time, restriction, and density — all defining qualities of bone. Cornell (1933): 'Saturn rules the skeletal system, the bones, the joints, the teeth.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: All bones, all joints, tooth enamel, cartilage, ligaments
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell (1933): 'Saturn rules everything that structures and lasts.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Acute bone trauma, fractures, inflammatory arthritis
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Mars in Saturn-ruled signs → fracture risk.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Bone growth, hip bones, marrow expansion
Spine structure, vitality of bone, vitamin D activation
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'The Sun in Leo rules the spine.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Bone marrow (Pluto modern attribution overlaps), bone mineral fluid balance
Lumbar spine, beauty of bone structure, joint smoothness
Spinal nerves emerging from bone, fine bones of hands
Sudden fractures, joint dislocations, bone-electrical anomalies

astrologicalRulership.fixedCrossInfluence: Less direct than for soft tissues. Capricorn (cardinal earth) is the primary skeletal sign.
secondarySigns: Each sign rules specific bones per medical astrology body map: Aries (skull), Taurus (cervical vertebrae C1-C7), Gemini (clavicle, shoulder blade, ribs, arm bones), Cancer (sternum, breast bone), Leo (thoracic spine, heart bone), Virgo (mid-back, lower thoracic), Libra (lumbar, kidney region), Scorpio (pelvis, sacrum), Sagittarius (hips, femur), Capricorn (knees, patella), Aquarius (calf bones — tibia/fibula), Pisces (feet bones — tarsals)

axes.name: Emotional-Structural Axis
axes.interpretation: Cancer (emotional, fluid) opposite Capricorn (structural, dense). Bone density loss often tracks chronic emotional weight not metabolized. Calcium leaches from bone under chronic cortisol (Saturn) excess.

axes.name: Renal Axis (calcium-skeletal overlay)
axes.interpretation: Kidney function affects calcium regulation (active vitamin D conversion). Renal-skeletal axis important for bone health.

energetics.nervousSystemPathway: Spinal cord housed within vertebral column — direct relationship
energetics.endocrineCorrespondence: Parathyroid (calcium regulation), thyroid (calcitonin), pituitary (growth hormone), gonads (estrogen/testosterone — bone density)
energetics.meridianCrossover_TCM: Kidney meridian (governs bones in TCM — 'Kidney rules bones'). Bladder meridian along spine.

cellSalts.indicatedFor: Bone density, tooth health, slow bone healing, growing pains, calcium-phosphorus balance. Foundation cell salt.
Ligament laxity, joint instability, dental enamel, surface of bones, varicose patterns.
Bone covering (periosteum), joint cartilage, nerve sheath protection.
Cramping from low magnesium (affects bone calcium uptake too).

supplementaryHerbs.name: Horsetail (Equisetum)
supplementaryHerbs.mechanism: High in silica — supports bone, hair, nails, connective tissue
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Brittle bones, weak nails, hair loss

supplementaryHerbs.name: Cissus quadrangularis
supplementaryHerbs.mechanism: Ayurvedic bone-strengthening herb — supports osteoblast activity
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Bone healing, fracture recovery, osteoporosis support

crystals.name: Onyx
crystals.bodyPlacement: Base of spine, hold during meditation
crystals.classicalAttribution: Saturn stone — strengthens resolve and structure

crystals.name: Apatite
crystals.classicalAttribution: Calcium phosphate composition — direct bone resonance

modalityRecommendations.individual: Bone health is built through: weight-bearing exercise (walking, dancing, jumping, strength training), adequate protein, calcium + magnesium + D + K2 (the bone matrix nutrients), avoiding excess alcohol/smoking, sleep, addressing chronic stress (cortisol leaches calcium from bone).
modalityRecommendations.medical_astrologer: Saturn + Capricorn primary. Saturn-Moon for hormonal-bone interaction. Sign-by-sign bone attribution. Saturn return (28-30, 58-60) often manifests bone events. Long Saturn transits to natal placements warrant bone density attention.
modalityRecommendations.reiki: Root chakra grounding extended. Bone-direct intention. Distance reiki appropriate for elderly fragile clients.
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_general: Spinal mobilization (gentle, age-appropriate). Joint mobilization. Myofascial release affecting bone alignment. NEVER force a stuck joint.
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_contraindications: Osteoporosis: NO HIGH-VELOCITY MANIPULATION (HVLA). Recent fracture: refer. Suspected fracture: refer. Bone metastasis history: gentle techniques only with medical clearance. Pregnancy: trained prenatal only for lumbar/pelvic work.
modalityRecommendations.naturopathy: Calcium (food-first — dark greens, sardines, sesame), magnesium glycinate, vitamin D3 (test 25(OH)D, target 50-80 ng/mL), vitamin K2 (MK-7), boron, silica (horsetail), strontium (controversial), weight-bearing exercise prescription.
modalityRecommendations.acupuncture: Kidney meridian (KD 3 Taixi — Kidney rules bones in TCM). Bladder back-shu points along spine. Du Mai for spinal support.
modalityRecommendations.ayurveda_phase2_placeholder: Asthi Dhatu (bone tissue — 5th of 7 dhatus). Majja inside bone (marrow → nervous tissue). Asthi tonics. Phase 2.
modalityRecommendations.pastoral_spiritual: Bones as the body's memory. Ancestral connection through bone. 'May this body remember its strength.'

pathologies.name: Osteoporosis / Osteopenia
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Atrophy of bone — Saturn afflictions over time; deficiency of bone-building cell salts.' Carey: 'Calc Phos deficiency = bone weakness.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Bone health benefits from weight-bearing exercise (most important), adequate calcium + D + K2 + magnesium, sufficient protein, and addressing risk factors (smoking, excess alcohol, low body weight). DEXA scan after age 50 (women) or 70 (men) gives baseline; earlier if risk factors.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn-Moon and Saturn-Venus configurations classically align with bone density loss patterns. Modern: DEXA T-score, FRAX risk assessment. Calcium, D3, K2, magnesium, strontium, boron supportive. Refer for endocrinology/rheumatology — bisphosphonates, denosumab, parathyroid hormone analogs may be appropriate.

modalityNotes.bodywork: NO HVLA manipulation. Gentle mobilization only. Postural work to reduce fall risk.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Weight-bearing + resistance exercise (most evidence-based). Adequate protein. Comprehensive bone nutrient stack. Fall prevention. Address contributing factors.

redFlags: Suspected vertebral compression fracture (sudden back pain after minor trauma in elderly) → urgent imaging

pathologies.name: Osteoarthritis
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Saturn in joints → restriction, hardening, degenerative change over time.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Joint pain that worsens with use and improves with rest, morning stiffness < 30 min — gentle movement is medicine. Strengthening surrounding muscles, maintaining healthy weight, addressing biomechanics, anti-inflammatory diet, glucosamine/chondroitin (modest evidence), turmeric all supportive. Severe pain or joint deformity → orthopedics.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn-cardinal sign configurations classically associated with degenerative joint patterns. Differentiate OA (mechanical) from RA (autoimmune — different signature). Boswellia, turmeric/curcumin, omega-3, glucosamine sulfate, MSM all evidence-supported adjuncts. Refer for orthopedics for severe joint destruction (joint replacement consideration).

modalityNotes.bodywork: Gentle joint mobilization, surrounding muscle release, fascial work. Avoid aggressive on inflamed joint.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Movement (not rest — joints need motion to stay healthy), weight management, anti-inflammatory diet, supplements above.

redFlags: Hot red swollen joint with fever → rule out septic arthritis (ED)
Sudden severe pain disproportionate to history → urgent evaluation

pathologies.name: Fracture (Acute)

planetaryCombinations: Mars transit through natal Saturn (or vice versa)

pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Fractures — Mars-Saturn afflictions; Mars-Uranus for sudden traumatic fracture.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Suspected fracture (severe pain, swelling, deformity, inability to use the part) requires urgent medical evaluation and imaging. Healing support: protein, calcium, magnesium, vitamin D, vitamin C (collagen), vitamin K2, comfrey topical (root, not internal).
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Mars-Saturn transits classically associated with fracture timing. Acute: refer for imaging/orthopedics. Healing phase: support with nutrient stack above. Comfrey (Symphytum, classical bone-knit) topical for closed fractures. AVOID stimulating modalities over acute fracture.

modalityNotes.bodywork: AVOID over acute fracture. Recovery phase (post-immobilization): gentle scar tissue work, joint mobilization, surrounding muscle work for strength rebuilding.
modalityNotes.reiki: Excellent for healing support — non-touching distance reiki ideal for casted limbs.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Comprehensive bone nutrient stack. Protein adequacy. Avoid NSAIDs (impair bone healing — evidence). Movement of unaffected areas to maintain conditioning.

redFlags: Loss of pulse distal to fracture → ED (vascular compromise)

pathologies.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA)

transitTriggers: Outer planet transits to natal Saturn

pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Rheumatism — Saturn-Mars-Mercury afflictions; chronic inflammatory joint pattern.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Symmetric joint pain (especially small joints of hands/feet) with morning stiffness > 30 min and fatigue requires rheumatology evaluation. RA is a serious autoimmune condition requiring medical management.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Mars-Saturn-Neptune complexes classically associated with autoimmune inflammatory joint patterns. RA is rheumatology territory — DMARDs, biologics. Holistic adjuncts: anti-inflammatory diet (Mediterranean, autoimmune protocol), omega-3, turmeric, vitamin D, gut health (gut-joint axis), stress management. NEVER replace rheumatology care.

modalityNotes.bodywork: Active flare: gentle only. Remission: more active work appropriate. Always within client tolerance.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: AIP (autoimmune protocol) diet trial. Anti-inflammatory foods. Address gut health. Stress management. Movement within tolerance.

redFlags: Cervical spine RA involvement → urgent (atlantoaxial instability)
Vasculitis features (skin, eyes, lungs) → urgent rheumatology

pathologies.name: Lumbar Disc Herniation
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Lumbar region — Saturn in Libra; disc + nerve compression patterns.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Acute disc-pattern pain (often with sciatic radiation down the leg) typically improves with: avoiding aggravating positions, gentle movement (walking), nerve glides, and time. Severe weakness, bowel/bladder symptoms, or saddle anesthesia require immediate medical evaluation.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn-Mars in Libra/Scorpio classically associated with lumbar disc patterns. Most disc herniations resolve with conservative care (movement, PT, time). Imaging (MRI) only changes management for surgical candidates or red flags. Refer for ortho/neuro if red flags or refractory > 6 weeks.

modalityNotes.bodywork: AVOID flexion-rotation forces. Gentle traction, nerve glides, surrounding musculature. McKenzie extension approach for many disc patterns.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Movement (not bed rest). McGill big 3 (curl-up, side plank, bird dog) for spine stability. Hip mobility. Address ergonomics.

vedicOverlay.ayurvedicTreatments_phase2: Asthi rasayanas. Lakshadi guggulu. Cissus quadrangularis. Mahanarayana taila external. Phase 2.

bodywork.contraindicationsCriticallity: HIGH — osteoporosis, fracture, RA cervical, anticoagulants

complianceNotes.scopeOfPractice: Bone density assessment requires DEXA (medical). Fracture care is medical/orthopedic. RA is rheumatology. Holistic adjuncts complement; never replace.
criticalReferralRules: Bowel/bladder dysfunction with back pain → ED (cauda equina)
Hot red swollen joint + fever → ED (septic arthritis)
Suspected vertebral compression in elderly → urgent imaging
